_note: One composition recipe per visual format (keys match taxonomies.json formats). Each inherits `family` (from archetype-families.json) and overrides format-specific bits. Distilled from the visual-formats skill. Missing fields fall back to the family default.

AI Slop Animation.composition: A surreal, AI-generated scene that personifies the product or dramatizes its invisible mechanism as one bold visual metaphor; a single striking central subject; clean space for the headline.

ASMR.composition: Extreme sensory close-up of the product's texture or action (pour, scoop, absorb, glide); macro detail that makes the sensation almost tactile; minimal text.

Behind the Scenes.composition: A candid insider view of how the product is made, sourced, or packed; real people at work; documentary framing; product in context, not hero-posed.

Billboard.composition: Styled as a large outdoor billboard within a real public setting (highway, subway, bus stop). One bold message and a large product/hero; lots of clean space; the OOH context signals scale and legitimacy.

Case Study.composition: One customer outcome told with a metric or simple chart element; a clean data zone plus a person or product anchor in the opposite corner.

ChatGPT.composition: Mimic the ChatGPT interface; the user's typed question mirrors the customer's real question, and the answer reveals the product naturally.

Cinematic B-Roll.composition: A high-production cinematic still of an aspirational moment; the product woven into a beautiful, atmospheric world; dramatic but tasteful.

Comment Response.composition: A surfaced social comment pinned at the top with the brand/creator's reply below it; feels like a native in-app reply.

Demo.composition: The product shown mid-use, demonstrating its function; hands-on; the performance moment (the visible result of using it) is the focus.

Expert Explainer.composition: An authority figure (doctor, trainer, formulator) mid-explanation in a credible setting; the product or ingredient used as a visual aid; trust-forward.

Feature Benefit Callout.composition: The product centered with 2+ clean callout labels/arrows pointing to specific features or benefits; ample margin around the product for the callouts; label legible.

Founder.composition: The founder speaking directly in an authentic setting (workshop, kitchen, office); personal and trust-building; the product nearby, not hero-posed.

Greenscreen.composition: A speaker cut out and composited over a background screenshot/website/graphic they are reacting to; the background content is the evidence, the speaker provides the frame.

Grid Swap.composition: A uniform multi-cell grid with consistent boxes showing variety, steps, or reasons; balanced, rhythmic layout.

How-To.composition: A clear numbered-step layout showing how to use the product; sequential structure; each step legible.

Letter.composition: A handwritten or typed personal letter addressed to the reader; intimate; the words are the focus; warm paper texture.

Listicle.composition: A numbered or bulleted list of benefits/tips in digestible chunks; clean type hierarchy; the product or an icon set alongside.

Meme.composition: A recognizable evergreen meme template with the brand mapped onto its existing meaning; native, humorous, low-fi.

Native Text Overlay.composition: A casual phone-shot photo with native-style caption text as the hook; UGC aesthetic; top or bottom reserved for the overlay.

Nostalgia.composition: A retro / era-specific aesthetic (90s, Y2K) evoking sentimentality; period props and color grade; the product woven into the throwback scene.

Notes App.composition: An Apple Notes screenshot with unfiltered personal thoughts or a confession; the text is the focus; believable Notes UI chrome.

One Shot.composition: A single striking still with one sentence of overlay; the visual is the hook and the copy is the payoff; stillness itself is the pattern interrupt.

Pattern Interrupt.composition: A surreal, unexpected, or absurd image that is somehow connected to the product's promise; one bold focal oddity designed to stop the scroll; clean space for the headline.

Podcast.composition: A multi-mic podcast set with two speakers mid-conversation; earned, conversational, studio-lit but relaxed.

Post It.composition: A sticky note with a short handwritten message shown to camera; informal, punchy, single idea.

Press.composition: Press logos, a headline, or a quote from a reputable outlet as the hero credibility element; clean and authoritative; product small.

Reaction.composition: Capture a genuine first-impression reaction — expression mid-surprise or delight — to a result; authentic, unposed.

Review.composition: A real customer review as the central element — the review card / star rating / quote is the hero; the product appears small.

Selfie.composition: An informal arm's-length or mirror selfie of the user; casual, in-feed; the product held in hand or visible in frame.

Sign.composition: A person holding (or an image featuring) a bold sign / poster / whiteboard with the single USP or CTA; direct and legible.

Skit.composition: A staged, relatable scenario captured mid-scene with characters; the product resolves the story; the moment is recognizable.

Social Proof Mashup.composition: A compilation grid of several UGC talking-head stills; the volume and variety of voices is the hook.

Split Screen.composition: The canvas cleanly split — subject or scene on one side, a supporting graphic or a second reality on the other; balanced and intentional.

Street Interview.composition: A vox-pop street-interview moment; interviewer and responder in a public setting; impromptu, in-the-wild, real.

Text Message.composition: An SMS / chat-thread mockup — a friend recommending the product; feels private and unfiltered; believable messaging UI.

Time Lapse.composition: A multi-frame progression showing gradual change over time as a sequence; the speed of progress is the hook. If it depicts a person's body, avoid any medical before/after implication.

Trend.composition: Adopts a current trending format or parody; culturally fluent and native to the feed; the product rides the trend.

Try-On.composition: The product worn or applied on-body to show fit, feel, or look; focus on how it appears in use.

Unboxing.composition: The packaging opening / reveal moment; anticipation and premium presentation; the product emerging from its box.

Unconventional Text Placement.composition: The headline placed in an unexpected physical location (on skin, an object, a surface) that is relevant to the message; the surprising placement is the hook.

Us vs. Them.composition: A side-by-side comparison against the old way or a well-known alternative (not a named competitor unless the brand permits); the brand wins the comparison obviously and honestly.

VSL.composition: A compelling opening still / thumbnail for a long-form sales video: a bold hook visual plus the product, engineered for intrigue and a click.

Yapper.composition: A single-take, direct-to-camera talking-head still; one speaker, one environment; authentic, personality-led.
